steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content:
Before the blank: “Puerto Rico's” (a possessive phrase needing a noun to complete it)
After the blank: “showcase” (a verb, beginning the predicate)
The blank is filled with museums, completing the subject noun phrase “Puerto Rico's museums”.

steps.title: Apply the rule for subject-verb punctuation
steps.content:
The rule is: a subject noun phrase and its verb must not be separated by punctuation.

Choice A uses no punctuation, correctly connecting the subject “Puerto Rico's museums” to the verb “showcase”.

Choices B, C, and D all insert punctuation (colon, comma, em dash) between the subject and verb, violating this fundamental rule.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
